steps.title: Identify sin X from the triangle
steps.content:
From angle $X$: opposite side = $ZY = 35$ and hypotenuse = $XY$.
By definition, $\sin X = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{35}{XY}$.

steps.title: Apply the complementary angle identity
steps.content:
In any right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary (sum to $90°$).
The cofunction identity: when two angles are complementary, the sine of one angle equals the cosine of the other.
Since angles $X$ and $Y$ are complementary: $$\sin X = \cos Y$$

Verification: From angle $Y$, adjacent side = $ZY = 35$ and hypotenuse = $XY$, so $\cos Y = \frac{35}{XY} = \sin X$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
